note: worktree was dirty at task creation

Decision: changes_requested (revision 1). Independent validation passed typecheck and 29/29 application, 70/70 source, 15/15 algorithm, 30/30 schema; guard:worktree, diff hygiene, and protected paths are clean. Passing tests do not satisfy the brief and actual code has blocking contract defects. 1) The public ComparisonApplicationContext exports arbitrary qualityPolicy injection; any releaseStatus other than not_released opens creation, there is no released quality evaluator using immutable source inputs, evidence is always marked ready/eligible, and null overallScore is persisted as zero. Keep the success profile internal/test-only and fail closed on unpublished/incomplete policy or score. 2) Create has no pre-source idempotency check, so replay rereads sources; fingerprint omits contract checksum; request display/version/period facts are trusted and persisted without binding them to resolved workspace/object/snapshot facts; date validation accepts impossible calendar dates; generated UUIDs/clock and source timestamps are not validated; source errors can expose provider messages. 3) Core graph has no required pre/post cross-row recomputation or fault hooks/tests, and there is no audit_event write for failures. Add every-stage rollback, two-connection race, source/quality/algorithm zero-write, audit-minimization, and tamper tests. 4) Automatic rule generation failure creates no failed Attempt/Outcome; create swallows all errors; ensure-once ignores interrupted attempts, replay cannot repair a missing automatic explanation, manifest ownership/checksum validation imports are unused, and explicit failure stores raw error.message. Implement transactionally allocated sequence, controlled failed outcome, interrupted semantics, ensure-once/retry/race tests, and same-Run manifest verification. 5) Archive replay compares only operation rather than stored operation_fingerprint, so changed actor/reason/expectation can replay; expected sequence is absent from input/fingerprint; add exact fingerprint, optimistic sequence, collision and concurrency coverage. 6) Read model silently filters malformed JSON, returns null or blank display names for corrupted aggregates, lacks cursor/limit validation and post-read ownership/sequence/score checks. Fail closed with controlled errors and tamper tests. The handoff also claims interrupted explanation and archive replay conflict coverage that the 29 tests do not contain, and incorrectly says domain memory was missing although brief gives its canonical path. Out-of-scope dirty paths are accepted only for Task Bus transition and not attributed to T0040. Memory Used: Do not trust PRD or subagent summaries for falsifiable external-repo facts affected source-versus-handoff inspection; External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary affected resolved-source validation requirements; Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission affected the test-claim comparison. Metadata updated to use_count 30, 12, 18; last_used_at 2026-07-19; expires_at 2026-10-17. Memory Candidates are generic/incomplete and not promoted.

Decision: changes_requested (revision 2). Independent validation passed typecheck and 31/31 application, 70/70 source, 15/15 algorithm, 30/30 schema; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths are clean. Several revision-1 blockers remain or were closed against the wrong contract. 1) ComparisonApplicationContext is still exported from application/index.ts and the top-level index, and it still publicly contains _qualityPolicy, _faultHook, algorithmConfig, clock, and uuid. An underscore/comment is not an access boundary; the new test merely asserts that a different property name qualityPolicy is absent. Move released policy/evaluator, algorithm config, clock/UUID and fault injection behind a non-exported internal/test factory, while the production composition can only use getProductionQualityPolicy and cannot open Run creation. The current evaluator also accepts any releaseStatus other than not_released and does not evaluate immutable quality inputs or validate a released policy contract. 2) validateRequestBinding only checks objectId, snapshotId and periods. It does not bind resolved.workspaceId, dataVersion, top-level versus snapshot sourceSystem/contractVersion, generatedAt, or source metadata; family/type/displayName remain trusted request values. isUtcTimestampMs accepts impossible timestamps such as 2026-02-30, confirmed by direct Node probe normalizing it to 2026-03-02. Add complete source binding and strict round-trip timestamp/calendar tests. 3) validateGraphInvariants only checks counts, workspace on three collections and finite score. It does not verify participant run ownership, source-to-participant bijection, same source contract, evidence role/participant ownership, candidate keys, exact units, assessment FKs, coverage/score/delta/contribution recomputation, checksums, or a post-insert projection. No audit_event writes exist. Fault coverage has only one after-whole-graph insert rollback, no per-table stages, two-connection idempotency/attempt/archive race, audit minimization, raw SQLite wrapping, or tamper tests. 4) Explanation lifecycle contradicts the brief: Attempt and Outcome are inserted in one transaction, so the product cannot produce a real interrupted attempt; the test manufactures one by deleting a prior outcome, then mutates that historical attempt by adding a new outcome. An interrupted attempt must remain interrupted, and an explicit retry must append a new sequence. Completed failed outcomes currently short-circuit createExplanation, so retry is impossible. Automatic manifest ownership validation is still absent, and persistFailedExplanation swallows persistence failure without audit/log. 5) Archive still has no expected event sequence in its input/fingerprint and no real concurrent-sequence test. Read-model JSON parsers still catch malformed or wrong-shaped JSON and return/filter to empty arrays, the opposite of fail closed; detail validation only checks two display names, list does not validate cursor pair/timestamp/runId or archiveFilter, and no aggregate ownership/sequence/recomputed-value tamper tests exist. 6) Handoff says docs/notes-backend.md was updated for revision 2 and 31 tests, but the actual file still reports 29/29 and claims interrupted coverage from revision 1. Reconcile notes and final handoff with actual tests. Out-of-scope dirty paths are accepted only for Task Bus transition and are not attributed to T0040. Memory Used: Do not trust PRD or subagent summaries for falsifiable external-repo facts affected direct source and timestamp verification; External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary affected complete source binding and corrupt-read requirements; Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission affected the 31-test and notes comparison. Metadata updated to use_count 31, 13, 19; last_used_at 2026-07-19; expires_at 2026-10-17. Memory Candidates remain incomplete/generic and are not promoted.

Decision: changes_requested (revision 3). Independent validation passed typecheck and 32/32 application, 70/70 source, 15/15 algorithm, 30/30 schema; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths are clean. Revision 3 closes strict timestamp validation, adds expectedSequence to archive, and improves explicit explanation retry, but the handoff itself admits critical blockers remain and several revision-2 requirements were not implemented. 1) The exported ComparisonApplicationContext still exposes algorithmConfig, clock, uuid, _qualityPolicy, and _faultHook through both application/index.ts and the top-level index. A caller can still inject any non-not_released releaseStatus and open Run creation; no released-policy validator/evaluator exists. This must be a genuinely non-exported internal/test composition, with a separate production composition that cannot bypass getProductionQualityPolicy. 2) Source binding still fails open when listPortraitObjects does not return the object: family/objectType and displayName remain trusted request facts. It also lacks negative contract tests for workspace, dataVersion, top-level/snapshot system and contract, timestamp, duplicate/missing object, and source metadata binding. Missing source authority must fail closed, not fall back to request values. 3) validateGraphInvariants remains far short of the brief: it does not prove one source per participant, evidence-to-role binding, assessment evidence FK ownership, exact candidate uniqueness/completeness, included/excluded field equivalence, or recompute normalized values, deltas, similarity, contributions, coverage and overall score with tolerance. There is still no post-insert projection validation, tamper suite, or per-table fault injection. 4) audit_event is still never written for source/quality/algorithm/transaction/explanation failures. Raw transaction/fault errors are still rethrown, and no minimal-metadata audit tests exist. No two-connection idempotency, explanation-sequence, or archive-sequence race test exists despite comments claiming race coverage. 5) Read model still catches malformed/wrong-shaped JSON and silently returns or filters to empty arrays; detail validation still checks only blank display names; cursor pair/timestamp/runId and runtime archiveFilter remain unvalidated. This is not fail closed and has no corruption/tamper tests. 6) Automatic explanation path still lacks same-Run manifest ownership validation. The new two-transaction explicit path reads MAX(sequence) again after committing the Attempt, so a concurrent attempt can make the returned attemptSequence belong to another attempt; return the allocated sequence from the Attempt transaction and test real concurrency. Only one test was added between revisions 2 and 3, so the handoff claim that complete binding and strengthened invariants are now covered is unsupported. Out-of-scope dirty paths are accepted only for Task Bus transition and are not attributed to T0040. Decision: changes_requested (revision 4). Independent validation passed typecheck and 32/32 application, 70/70 source, 15/15 algorithm, 30/30 schema; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths are clean. Revision 4 fixes missing-object source fail-closed and returns allocated explanation sequences, but most revision-3 blockers remain. 1) The new public composition is not usable: exported create/list/detail/explanation/archive functions still require ComparisonApplicationContext, whose required algorithmConfig is absent from ComparisonApplicationPublicContext. The internal alias remains exported from comparison-application.ts and accepts any releaseStatus other than not_released. Add a real production factory/wrapper that accepts only public dependencies and always uses getProductionQualityPolicy, while keeping the released test profile internal and validated. 2) validateGraphInvariants still does not prove one source per role, assessment-evidence role ownership, candidate uniqueness/completeness, included/excluded field equivalence, or recompute normalized values, deltas, similarities, contributions, coverage and overall score with tolerance; there is no post-insert projection validation or tamper suite. 3) audit_event is never written. There are still no per-table rollback hooks/tests or real two-connection idempotency, attempt-sequence and archive-sequence races; the single after-whole-graph fault test does not satisfy the brief. 4) The handoff claim that malformed JSON now throws ComparisonStateError is false: JSON.parse SyntaxError is unwrapped, and wrong element types are still silently filtered. Detail still returns null for corrupted participant/source cardinality and validates neither ownership, sequence, finite numbers nor recomputed values; cursor pair/timestamp/runId/archiveFilter remain runtime-unvalidated. 5) Automatic explanation still persists the rule manifest without validating every referenced record against the same workspace/run or recomputing its checksum from the stored canonical manifest. comparisonRecordExists is used only by the explicit explanation path. The test list remains 32 and contains no coverage for these mandatory cases. The revision-4 handoff also omits the required constraint matrix, V005 table mapping, contract-difference evidence, and detailed rollback/competition/gate evidence. Out-of-scope dirty paths are accepted only for Task Bus transition and are not attributed to T0040. Decision: changes_requested (revision 5). Independent validation passed typecheck and 34/34 application, 70/70 source, 15/15 algorithm, 30/30 schema; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths are clean. The production factory and JSON.parse error wrapping are useful fixes, but the revision adds only two factory tests and leaves mandatory revision-4 blockers unresolved. 1) validateGraphInvariants still checks only basic counts, ownership, units and finite run fields. It does not prove source-role bijection, assessment evidence role/FK binding, candidate uniqueness/completeness, included/excluded equivalence, or recompute normalized values, deltas, similarity, contribution, coverage and overall score with tolerance. There is no post-insert projection validation or tamper suite. 2) No audit_event/writeAudit path exists. There are no per-table rollback stages and no real two-connection idempotency, explanation-sequence or archive-sequence tests; comments about transaction rechecks are not concurrency evidence. Raw transaction/fault failures can still escape rather than producing controlled errors plus minimal audit metadata. 3) Filtering non-string/non-object elements from persisted JSON is silent corruption, contrary to the brief requirement to validate JSON shape and fail closed. Detail still returns null for corrupted participant/source cardinality and does not validate aggregate ownership, sequences, finite fields or recomputed values. Cursor pair, timestamp, runId and runtime archiveFilter validation are still absent, with no malformed/wrong-shape/tamper tests. 4) Automatic explanation claims ensure-once including interrupted, but both replay and generate paths only treat attempts with non-null outcomes as existing. An interrupted automatic Attempt therefore causes another automatic Attempt instead of remaining interrupted until explicit retry. Manifest checks only pre-insert record existence; it does not recompute/compare checksum from the exact canonical JSON to be stored, recheck existence inside the Attempt transaction, or test tampered/cross-run manifests and concurrency. 5) The handoff constraint matrix overstates implementation and its key negative evidence omits every mandatory category above. W06 remains blocked. Out-of-scope dirty paths are accepted only for Task Bus transition and are not attributed to T0040. Decision: changes_requested (revision 9). Independent validation passed typecheck and 48/48 application, 70/70 source, 15/15 algorithm, 30/30 schema; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths are clean. Revision 9 changes only tests, and five of six new tests do not exercise the application failure branches their names claim. 1) The three tests named corrupted detail throws ComparisonStateError never call getComparisonDetail after corruption; they assert raw SQLite CHECK/FK/UNIQUE errors. The non-finite test only asserts a normal score is finite, and the sequence test only asserts a normal first sequence equals one. These are not negative read-model tests. 2) The added idempotency test duplicates an existing single-connection pre-source conflict test and explicitly leaves that conflict unaudited. It is not the required two-connection race. 3) No implementation or evidence was added for exact graph/evidence-role equivalence, numerical recomputation with tolerance, post-insert projection validation, per-table rollback, cursor validation, validation/explanation audit, controlled wrapping of algorithm/SQLite errors, or transactional manifest ownership recheck. 4) Every application test still opens only one DatabaseSync connection, so real idempotency, attempt-sequence and archive-sequence competition remains untested. 5) The suite still lacks tampered/cross-run manifest checksum and ownership cases. Test names and comments must match actual assertions; SQLite schema rejection and happy-path reads cannot substitute for application corruption tests mandated by the brief. W06 remains blocked. Decision: changes_requested (revision 10). Independent validation passed typecheck and 50/50 application, 70/70 source, 15/15 algorithm, 30/30 schema; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths are clean. Revision 10 still changes only tests and does not close the required implementation gates. 1) Four tests whose names claim getComparisonDetail throws ComparisonStateError first have SQLite reject the UPDATE, then call getComparisonDetail on unchanged valid data and assert success. Their names remain false and they provide no application corruption evidence. The old happy-path finite and sequence tests also remain. 2) The two-connection test is sequential, not a race: connection A completes create and commits before connection B starts its call. It proves cross-connection visibility only, not the inside-transaction recheck under an overlapping competition window. 3) No graph numerical recomputation, post-insert projection validation, candidate/evidence-role tamper suite, per-table insert fault hooks, validation/explanation audit, controlled internal-error wrapping, strict cursor validation, transactional manifest ownership recheck, or attempt/archive sequence race was added. 4) The test suite provides no way to pause one writer after precheck/source resolution and release a competing writer, and no worker/thread or equivalent real overlap. 5) Schema constraint tests must be named and reported as schema defense tests; they cannot be presented as application fail-closed tests. W06 remains blocked. Out-of-scope dirty paths are accepted only for Task Bus transition and are not attributed to T0040. Decision: changes_requested (revision 16). Independent validation passed typecheck and unchanged 59/59 application, 70/70 source, 15/15 algorithm, 30/30 schema; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths are clean. Revision 16 contains no implementation or test change and only acknowledges the already recorded mandatory gaps, so it cannot satisfy the handoff. This should reuse or extract pure deterministic computation over the persisted projection inside the transaction; it does not authorize source I/O, rule generation, or a second external algorithm execution inside the transaction. Decision: changes_requested (revision 28). Independent validation passed typecheck and 75/75 application, 70/70 source, 15/15 algorithm, 30/30 schema; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths are clean. Revision 28 strengthens both-side quality assertions, but exposes and then incorrectly blesses a real approved-contract drift: 1) T0038 explicitly requires V005 evidence qualityStatus=limited alongside algorithm qualityEligibility=insufficient. mapEvidenceProjection correctly maps non-empty evidence sourceQualityFlags to limited/insufficient, but buildRunGraph persists comparison_dimension_evidence.quality_status from snapshot flags only, producing ready. Fix persistence to use the exact quality projection supplied to the algorithm, assert exact canonical source_flags_json (not substring matching), and expect both flagged evidence rows to be limited while unflagged rows remain ready. 2) explanation_generation_failed audit was added without any test. Schema constraints are not a waiver: add an internal/test-only rule-generation or explanation fault seam within allowed application/test paths and assert the actual failed Attempt/Outcome plus one minimal audit row and sanitized reason. The current audit is written only after failed Outcome persistence succeeds; explanation Attempt/Outcome persistence failures are still silently swallowed without audit evidence. Cover generation and persistence failure semantics required by the brief. 3) Input/mode/graph/post-insert validation audit remains absent; the revision-27 test still asserts zero audit for invalid input rather than closing the requested validation audit path. 4) Strict detail exact cardinality/relations, contiguous sequences, legal archive transitions, and recomputed score/coverage corruption remain unchanged. 5) Manifest same-run ownership/checksum rechecks remain incomplete and outside the Attempt insert transaction, and no tampered/cross-run/competition tests exist. 6) The only two-connection test remains sequential committed visibility; genuine overlapping create, attempt-sequence, and archive-sequence competition is still absent. W06 remains planned and blocked; do not create it. Out-of-scope dirty paths are accepted only for Task Bus transition and are not attributed to T0040. Decision: changes_requested (revision 30). Independent validation passed typecheck and 79/79 application, 70/70 source, 15/15 algorithm, 30/30 schema; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths are clean. Revision 30 validly proves two explanation behaviors: a before_rule_generation fault persists a failed Attempt/Outcome plus an explanation_generation_failed audit without rolling back the core Run, and an after_attempt_before_outcome fault leaves an interrupted Attempt while preserving the Run. These subcases are closed. Remaining blockers: 1) The declared third stage inside_outcome_transaction is not tested. Outcome transaction/persistence failure is still caught and silently leaves an interrupted attempt with no audit assertion. Add a real fault test and the required minimal persistence-failure audit semantics; strengthen the generation audit assertion to exact row count, workspace/actor/run, reason code, and absence of injected raw error text. 2) The test named "invalid mode writes audit_event with reason code" asserts audit_count=0. It provides the opposite evidence and leaves input/mode/graph/post-insert validation audit unimplemented. Correct the implementation and test or rename/remove unsupported claims only after the required audit contract is actually satisfied. 3) The new "exact cardinality" detail test is happy-path only. It does not corrupt storage or prove getComparisonDetail fail-closed; its baselineEvidence filter compares participantId to objectId and then falls back to array position, so it proves no ownership relation. Add genuine readable-corruption fixtures/seams for exact participant/source/evidence/assessment cardinality and run/role/FK relations, contiguous attempt/archive sequences starting at one, legal archive transitions, and recomputed score/coverage. 4) Automatic/explicit manifest same-run ownership/checksum checks remain outside/incomplete relative to the Attempt insert transaction, with no tampered/cross-run tests. 5) Genuine overlapping two-connection create idempotency, attempt-sequence, and archive-sequence competition remains absent; sequential visibility is insufficient. W06 remains planned and blocked; do not create it. Out-of-scope dirty paths are accepted only for Task Bus transition and are not attributed to T0040. Decision: changes_requested (revision 32). Independent validation passed typecheck and 81/81 application, 70/70 source, 15/15 algorithm, 30/30 schema; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths are clean. Revision 32 validly closes the basic outcome-transaction failure behavior: inside_outcome_transaction rolls back the Outcome, preserves an interrupted Attempt and core Run, and writes explanation_outcome_persistence_failed audit. Strengthen this proof with exact one-row count, actor, request/run id, and assertion that injected provider text is absent. Remaining blockers: 1) The new detail corruption test is the opposite of the brief. It disables FKs, moves all assessments to a fake Run so the repository query silently returns zero assessments, then asserts getComparisonDetail succeeds. This codifies fail-open data loss. Remove/invert this assertion: detail must detect missing/extra candidate assessments and throw ComparisonStateError. Add genuine readable-corruption tests for exact participant/source/evidence/assessment cardinality, run/role/FK relations, contiguous attempt/archive sequences starting at one, legal archive transitions, and recomputed score/coverage. Do not describe silent omission as graceful degradation. 2) Renaming the mode test in revision 31 corrected the false title but did not implement input/mode/graph/post-insert validation audit required by prior reviews. Zero audit remains contrary evidence. 3) Automatic and explicit manifest same-run ownership/checksum rechecks are still incomplete/outside the Attempt insert transaction; explicit retry still lacks checksum recomputation. Add tampered/cross-run tests and same-transaction checks. 4) Genuine overlapping two-connection competition remains absent for create idempotency, explanation attempt sequence, and archive event sequence; sequential committed visibility is insufficient. W06 remains planned and blocked; do not create it. Out-of-scope dirty paths are accepted only for Task Bus transition and are not attributed to T0040. Decision: changes_requested (revision 33). Independent validation passed typecheck and 81/81 application, 70/70 source, 15/15 algorithm, 30/30 schema; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths are clean. Revision 33 correctly reverses the prior fail-open assessment-loss test: deleting all assessments now makes getComparisonDetail throw ComparisonStateError, and zero evidence is also rejected. This closes the zero-row data-loss subcase. Remaining blockers: 1) Non-zero is not exact aggregate validation. Detail must compare the persisted assessment dimension-key set/cardinality to the released run's candidate contract, verify exact per-role evidence set and assessment-to-evidence role/FK relations, and reject missing or extra rows. The current assessment ownership loop is effectively unreachable for cross-run rows because the repository query filters comparison_run_id first. Participant/source cardinality corruption still returns null from the repository and is indistinguishable from not-found instead of throwing a corrupted-state error. Add genuine negative tests for each. 2) Sequence checks still prove only strictly increasing SQL order, not contiguous sequences starting at one; legal archive transitions, attempt-outcome consistency, and recomputed detail score/coverage remain unvalidated. 3) Input/mode/graph/post-insert validation audit remains absent; existing tests explicitly expect zero audit. 4) Automatic/explicit manifest same-run ownership/checksum validation is still incomplete/outside the Attempt insertion transaction, with no tampered/cross-run tests. 5) Genuine overlapping two-connection create idempotency, attempt sequence, and archive sequence competition remains absent. Also strengthen outcome persistence audit evidence to exact count, actor, run/request id, and no raw injected text as previously requested. W06 remains planned and blocked; do not create it. Out-of-scope dirty paths are accepted only for Task Bus transition and are not attributed to T0040. Memory Used: External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary affected acceptance of the zero-row detail fix but requirement for exact durable aggregate validation; Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission affected distinguishing reachable zero-row checks from filtered/unreachable ownership checks and unchanged blockers. Decision: changes_requested (revision 35). Independent validation passed: apps/server typecheck; application 111/111, source 70/70, algorithm 15/15, schema 30/30; root guard:worktree and git diff --check; protected paths clean. Accepted progress: exact assessment set and numerical recomputation, strict participant/source cardinality, contiguous attempt/archive sequences and legal archive transitions, sanitized validation audits, manifest checksum/ownership recheck inside Attempt transactions, and deterministic two-connection race-window tests are implemented. Remaining bounded blockers: 1) The claimed exact per-role evidence set is not enforced: validateDetailAggregateConsistency indexes every evidence row but never rejects dimension keys outside algorithmConfig.candidateDimensions. Add explicit unknown/extra evidence rejection and negative tests for each role. 2) Detail executes a workspace-global orphan outcome scan. An unrelated orphan outcome can make every healthy Run in the workspace unreadable. Aggregate validation must be scoped to the requested Run; remove this global coupling and add a two-Run test proving unrelated corruption does not poison a healthy detail. 3) The post-insert validation audit test directly throws ComparisonStateError from the fault hook. It does not corrupt persisted projection data and prove the real post-insert validator detects it. Tamper an inserted row in the hook, let the actual validator throw, then assert exactly one sanitized audit and eight-table rollback. 4) comparison-application.ts contains literal NUL bytes in role/dimension map keys, causing text tools to treat the TypeScript source as binary. Replace them with a readable collision-safe representation (prefer nested maps/structured keys) and verify no NUL remains. 5) The brief requires /agentops-handoff-self-audit before handoff, but no 7-point PASS artifact/evidence is recorded. Run it and include the PASS evidence in the next handoff. The Memory Candidate is not promoted because it lacks required lifecycle metadata (created_at, last_used_at, use_count, expires_at, status). W06 remains planned and blocked; do not create it. Out-of-scope dirty paths are accepted only for Task Bus transition and are not attributed to T0040. Memory Used: External data adapters must fail-closed at every contract boundary affected rejection of the incomplete exact evidence-set validation; Handoff claims must be verified against actual test evidence before submission affected the mismatch between the claimed post-insert validator coverage/self-audit and actual evidence. Metadata updated to use_count 45 and 51; last_used_at 2026-07-19; expires_at 2026-10-17.
